name: Nikita Context Protocol
summary: NCP is a source-backed joke interface for useful overlap with Nikita Kramar's public site: signals, likely resonant ideas, receipts, and WWNKD moves.
bestRead: Nikita seems most interested in leverage with taste: open web context, agent orchestration, proof loops, and human judgment.
useFor: show one small artifact instead of a pitch
doNotUseFor: turning the joke into a dossier
changing the visual theme without explicit approval
likelyResonantIdeas: NCP: a tiny agent-readable personal-site layer
Context Autopsy / Rule Doctor for failed agent runs
The anti-automation boundary: what agents should not touch
defaultOpener: I made a dumb thing called NCP because your site already reads like it wants to be useful to synthetic readers. What should a personal site expose to agents, and what should it absolutely refuse?
